title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I honestly thought you were gi. Don't try to be gi because you're Korean. You need to make as many grammatical errors [music] as possible. I watch for over 5 hours every day. Every day. That is truly natural English. Welcome back to lunch hour English. Today's guest teaches English at Google, RealClass, and more. And she is going to teach you how to overcome English in a fun and natural way.

That's why every class that I have feels very different. It's kind of personalized for each person. Have you had any experiences with some of your students where they opened up to you about how English changed their life every day? Yeah, [laughter] every day one of my students, she's a pediatrician and she texted me, Tina, like there are patients from other countries and in the past I would use my translator, but these days I just naturally speak English and can make jokes and interact with kids. So she said, "Wow, this is such a wonderful experience." There are a lot of students who have children and they tell me when their kids ask the questions, they would go like, "Ask your dad. I'm going to look it up." But now they answer, they give their kids answers in English. What is a Tina Coup pro tip for learning English? Find the one that you enjoy the most. If you're in a medical field, start watching medical TV series. What I did in the past is I always memorize at least three sentences every day and repeat the same sentences every day.

Thank you. [laughter] Thank you. I'm like, "No, no, no. More than that. More than those." And they're like, "I can't remember any of the sentences." Now, then that's where you're going to have to start. Even though you watch a lot of videos in English and you study with uh English books, it's about what you can say out loud. When you watch like English videos for an hour and you feel like, "Oh yeah, I studied English." Mm- [laughter] not yet. You got to think how many expressions remain in your mind. So for basic level students, they should repeat the same three sentences for a week. But for intermediate students, they can do more. Do not memorize just words. For example, I'm rooting for you. You should memorize words chunk by chunk. Root for.
